steps.description: Type option 1 to select DB2 system catalog on the DB2 Admin main menu
Press Enter to navigate to the System Catalog Objects screen
Type option D to select Databases on the System Catalog Objects screen
Type the database name or partial name in the Name filter field to locate the target database
Press Enter to display the matching Databases list
Type the T line command in the Sel field next to the target database to list its tables
Press Enter to navigate to the Tables, Views, and Aliases list for the selected database
Type the AL line command in the Sel field next to the target table to open the Alter Table screen
Press Enter to open the Alter Table options screen for the selected table
Type S in the selection field next to ADD COLUMN to choose the Add Column option
Press Enter to open the Add Column form
Type the name for the new column in the Column name field
Type the data type for the new column in the Column type field (e.g., VARCHAR, INTEGER, CHAR)
Type the data length for the new column in the Data length field (used for built-in types such as VARCHAR or CHAR)
Type Yes or No in the Allow nulls field to specify whether the column accepts null values (Yes or blank for nullable, No for NOT NULL)
Type a value in the WITH DEFAULT field to set the column default (Yes, No, L for SECLABEL, or an explicit default value; leave blank for no default)
steps.placeholder: [With Default: Yes, No, or explicit value]

steps.description: Press Enter to submit the Add Column definition and display the Change Management prompt
Type No in the Change Management prompt to skip change management and execute the ALTER TABLE statement immediately
Press Enter to confirm and execute the ALTER TABLE statement; the message ALTER stmt executed appears in the Command area
Press PF3 to return to the Alter Table main options screen
Press PF3 to return to the Tables, Views, and Aliases list, where the altered table now shows the updated column count
